description: Map current Dashboard routes, implemented views, placeholders, and removed human-lock/approval surface.
focus: app.tsx routes; implemented views; module placeholders; removed locks approval
findings: Current app has old top-level module routes topology/forensic/semantic/ledger, with forensic/semantic/ledger still ModulePlaceholder. Real views are topology, rules, timeline, history, doctor. Locks/Human Lock route and approve client/API are absent in current branch. This invalidates Approval as a current first-level theme.

description: Map REST/SSE contracts that can support the four-theme restructure without adding write/control-plane behavior.
findings: Existing client supports GET /api/rules, /api/rules/context, /api/ledger, /api/scan, /api/doctor, /api/history/state and POST /api/intent/annotate. There is no human-lock approve API. SSE events cover meta:updated, ledger:appended, drift:detected and legacy lock events may still appear in hook list but no lock page consumes them. Readiness can use getScan; Health can use getDoctor plus current connection state.

description: Translate analysis conclusions into a concrete four-theme navigation and content model for future UI design.
findings: Target IA should be four first-level themes: Readiness, Rules Explain, Timeline, Health. Readiness is new and read-only scan/setup status. Rules Explain absorbs topology/rules/semantic explain. Timeline absorbs intent timeline/history/ledger analysis and keeps annotation as audit note. Health absorbs doctor/forensic/audit/runtime/tooling boundary. Approval/Human Lock must not be modeled unless a new confirmation data source is introduced.
key_files: .workflow/.analysis/ANL-2026-04-26-web端能力保留新增讨论/conclusions.json;.workflow/.analysis/ANL-2026-04-26-web端能力保留新增讨论/discussion.md

description: Identify planning risks, non-goals, and verification points for handing UI restructure to Gemini.
focus: do not edit UI now; avoid control-plane writes; tests; i18n; placeholders; stale docs
findings: Main risks: reintroducing Approval/Human Lock language, exposing Web fix buttons, treating placeholder modules as implemented, or designing against stale docs. Verification should ensure no new command-execution POSTs, no human-lock nav, annotation stays Timeline, Readiness only calls getScan, Health only suggests CLI for fixes. Gemini should update i18n and tests when implementing, but this plan phase only records scope.
